confirmed (broad_st_west/greenwood_ave_south corner - Corrected 2026-07-02 (Rollo): the earlier street-view photo review (2026-07-02) misread this hardware as a short davit arm - the signals are actually mast-arm held. Still a direct visual observation, not a field survey.
): full-width mast-arm signal, pole at the real corner-fillet arc midpoint; the arm extends at a right angle to broad_st_west (parallel to its crosswalk, perpendicular to its travel direction), reaching roughly to mid-roadway (arm_length_ft=27.8, half of broad_st_west's real curb-to-curb width) - confirmed via street-view against a real example (NW corner's arm over West Broad St), not a diagonal reach toward the intersection center. Exactly which lane the head hangs over isn't surveyed.

): pedestrian head mounted on the same pole as the vehicle signal.
